core_concept: Basic Shapes: Circle, Square, Triangle, Rectangle
facts: A circle has a curved outline with no corners, unlike a square which has four straight sides and four corners.
A triangle has exactly three straight sides and three corners; a rectangle has four straight sides and four corners.
A square is a special kind of rectangle because it has four equal sides and four corners, but not all rectangles are squares.
A rectangle has two pairs of same-length straight sides; the shorter pair and the longer pair are different lengths unless it is a square.
misconceptions: A triangle and a rectangle both have four corners — learners may think all shapes with straight sides have the same number of corners.
Any shape with a curved outline is called a circle — learners may mistake an oval or a rounded blob for a circle because it is not pointy.
